title: sagina saginoides subsp. pyrenaica
intro: Sagina saginoides subsp. pyrenaica, also known as Pyrenean pearlwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a low-growing, mat-forming perennial that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, Pyrenean pearlwort can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
